text:
The web search confirms that the Seed-OSS open-source models project exists and has a dedicated GitHub repository at https://github.com/ByteDance-Seed/seed-oss. The official repository and related sources consistently show the citation format with "ByteDance Seed Team" as the author. The GitHub repository explicitly includes a BibTeX citation that states: "author={ByteDance Seed Team}".

The automated checker noted an author mismatch, claiming the "actual" author is "ByteDance-Seed (ByteDance-Seed)" instead of "ByteDance Seed Team." However, my web search shows that the official GitHub repository itself uses "ByteDance Seed Team" in its own BibTeX citation metadata. The checker's "actual" designation appears to be a misidentification—it is extracting the GitHub organization name rather than the proper author attribution that appears in the repository's official citation guidance.

This is a case where the automated checker has encountered metadata naming conventions and misinterpreted them. The repository belongs to the "ByteDance-Seed" GitHub organization, but the proper author attribution (as specified in the project's own BibTeX citation) is "ByteDance Seed Team." The title "Seed-OSS Open-Source Models" (with capitalization that matches web sources), the year 2025, the URL, and the author attribution all align with the dedicated source page.

EXPLANATION: The exact source exists with matching title and author attribution. The official GitHub repository provides a BibTeX citation using "ByteDance Seed Team" as the author, confirming the cited reference is real.
LINK: https://github.com/ByteDance-Seed/seed-oss
FOUND_TITLE: Seed-OSS Open-Source Models
FOUND_AUTHORS: ByteDance Seed Team
FOUND_YEAR: 2025
VERDICT: UNLIKELY
